text: How can you throw a ball as hard as you can and have it come back to you without bouncing off anything?
explanation: Throw the ball straight up! Gravity will bring it back down to you. No bouncing needed — it simply decelerates, stops, and falls back.

text: A man enters a dark room with a match, an oil lamp, and a candle. What does he light first?
explanation: He lights the match first! You need fire to light anything else, and the match is the source of that initial flame. This is a logic puzzle disguised as a riddle.

text: What gets wetter the more it dries?
explanation: A towel! As it dries things (absorbs water), it gets wetter.

text: If you have a bowl with six apples and you take away four, how many do you have?
explanation: You have 4 — because YOU took 4 apples. The bowl has 2, but you have 4.

text: A rooster sits on the peak of a roof. It lays an egg. Which way does the egg roll?
explanation: Roosters don't lay eggs — only hens do! The question is a trick.

text: What has hands but can't clap?
explanation: A clock has hands (hour and minute hands) but cannot clap.

text: Before Mt. Everest was discovered, what was the tallest mountain in the world?
explanation: Mt. Everest was still the tallest mountain — it just hadn't been discovered yet! Discovery doesn't change physical reality.
All 12 months have at least 28 days! The question doesn't ask which months have ONLY 28 days.

text: A plane crashes exactly on the border of the US and Canada. Where do you bury the survivors?
explanation: You don't bury SURVIVORS — they're alive! The question tries to distract you with the border detail.

text: Is it legal for a man to marry his widow's sister?
explanation: If a man has a widow, he's dead! Dead men can't marry anyone.

text: What can you hold in your right hand but never in your left?
explanation: Your left elbow! You can hold it in your right hand but physically cannot hold it in your left hand.

text: I speak without a mouth and hear without ears. I have no body, but I come alive with the wind. What am I?
explanation: An echo! It "speaks" (repeats sounds) without a mouth, "hears" (responds to sound) without ears, has no physical body, and travels through the air.

text: What disappears the moment you say its name?
explanation: Silence! The moment you say the word "silence" (or anything), the silence is broken.

text: How far can a dog run into a forest?
explanation: Halfway! After that, the dog is running OUT of the forest, not into it.

text: If there are 3 apples and you take away 2, how many apples do YOU have?
explanation: You have 2 apples — the ones you took!

text: What occurs once in a minute, twice in a moment, but never in a thousand years?
explanation: The letter "M"! Minute has one M, moment has two Ms, and "a thousand years" has zero Ms.

text: I am not alive, but I grow; I don't have lungs, but I need air; I don't have a mouth, but water kills me. What am I?
explanation: Fire! It "grows" (spreads), needs air (oxygen) to burn, and water extinguishes it.

text: A man rode into town on Friday, stayed 3 days, and left on Friday. How?
choices: He arrived on a Friday and left on a Monday called Friday
explanation: His horse's name is Friday! He rode INTO town on Friday (the horse), stayed 3 days, and rode out on Friday (the horse).
